---
title: "Chapter-By-Chapter Expansion Map"
---

## How To Read This Map

The detailed chapter expansion outlines are organized by course part:

- [Part I: Foundations](parts/part-i-foundations.qmd)
- [Part II: Core Bayesian Inference](parts/part-ii-core-bayesian-inference.qmd)
- [Part III: Computation](parts/part-iii-computation.qmd)
- [Part IV: Models](parts/part-iv-models.qmd)
- [Part V: Optimization and Diagnostics](parts/part-v-optimization-and-diagnostics.qmd)
- [Part VI: Software Workflows](parts/part-vi-software-workflows.qmd)
- [Part VII: Advanced Topics](parts/part-vii-advanced-topics.qmd)

Each part page is written as an expansion-ready authoring brief. For every chapter it lists:

- core questions;
- formal topics and definitions;
- derivations that must be shown in detail;
- computational labs and software targets;
- diagnostics and common pitfalls;
- exercises and reading suggestions.

## Status Of Content In This Repository

| Chapter | Status in this repository | Location |
|---|---|---|
| 1-2 | Detailed outline | Part I overview |
| 3 | Fully written sample chapter | [Bayes theorem chapter](chapters/foundations/bayes-theorem-and-updating.qmd) |
| 4-6 | Fully written chapters | [Part II chapters](parts/part-ii-core-bayesian-inference.qmd) |
| 7 | Fully written chapter | [Numerical integration chapter](chapters/computation/numerical-integration-and-approximation.qmd) |
| 8 | Detailed outline | Part III overview |
| 9 | Fully written sample chapter | [MCMC chapter](chapters/computation/markov-chain-monte-carlo.qmd) |
| 10-12 | Fully written chapters | [Part III chapters](parts/part-iii-computation.qmd) |
| 13-20 | Fully written chapters | [Part IV chapters](parts/part-iv-models.qmd) |
| 21-23 | Fully written chapters | [Part V chapters](parts/part-v-optimization-and-diagnostics.qmd) |
| 24-26 | Fully written chapters | [Part VI chapters](parts/part-vi-software-workflows.qmd) |
| 27-31 | Detailed outline | Part VII overview |

## Authoring Convention

To expand the remaining outline chapters into full textbook-style chapters, retain the following section order whenever it fits the material:

1. Chapter introduction and learning outcomes.
2. Motivation and intuition.
3. Formal setup and notation.
4. Main result or model structure.
5. Step-by-step derivation.
6. Worked analytic example.
7. Computational implementation.
8. Diagnostics and failure modes.
9. Exercises with solution sketches.
10. References and further reading.

## Cross-Cutting Themes To Preserve

As the site grows, every chapter should remain aligned with five global themes:

### Mathematical precision

Definitions and assumptions should be explicit. Approximation methods should always state the object being approximated and the mode of approximation error.

### Workflow discipline

Every model chapter should include some version of:

- prior design;
- posterior computation;
- predictive checking;
- sensitivity analysis;
- reporting.

### Computational transparency

When algorithms are introduced, the course should state:

- target distribution;
- transition rule or optimization objective;
- tuning parameters;
- computational cost;
- known pathologies.

### Reproducibility

Every substantial code example should have a corresponding source file in `labs/`, or a direct path for students to reproduce it.

### Comparative reasoning

Students should repeatedly compare:

- likelihood vs posterior inference;
- optimization vs integration;
- MCMC vs VI;
- exact conjugate inference vs numerical approximation;
- prediction vs explanation vs decision.

## Immediate Reading Paths

Different audiences can enter the material through different routes.

### Statistically mature reader

Start with:

1. [Part III: Computation](parts/part-iii-computation.qmd)
2. [MCMC chapter](chapters/computation/markov-chain-monte-carlo.qmd)
3. [Stan workflow chapter](chapters/software/stan-hierarchical-models.qmd)
4. [Diagnostics section in Part V](parts/part-v-optimization-and-diagnostics.qmd)

### Applied modeler

Start with:

1. [Part II: Core Bayesian Inference](parts/part-ii-core-bayesian-inference.qmd)
2. [Part IV: Models](parts/part-iv-models.qmd)
3. [Part VI: Software Workflows](parts/part-vi-software-workflows.qmd)

### Theory-oriented reader

Start with:

1. [Part I: Foundations](parts/part-i-foundations.qmd)
2. [Bayes theorem chapter](chapters/foundations/bayes-theorem-and-updating.qmd)
3. [Part II: Core Bayesian Inference](parts/part-ii-core-bayesian-inference.qmd)
